spec.reasoning: I chose lexical renaming and a distractor column. For lexical: 'Person' → 'Client', 'Car' → 'Model', 'Value' → 'Amount' are all same-descriptiveness-level full-word renames. Sheet names 'Extract' → 'Source Data' and 'Desired Result' → 'Desired Output' are similarly descriptive. Note that 'Person' appears both as a header in both sheets, so both get renamed to 'Client' consistently (via column_rename_map applying to all sheets). The car model names (Mondeo, Giulia, etc.) in the Desired Result headers also appear as data values in the 'Car'/'Model' column of Extract — but since we are renaming 'Car' to 'Model' (not renaming the individual car names themselves), no data_value_renames are needed for those. The distractor column 'Category' is plausibly related to the data (could be a vehicle category) but clearly different from existing columns 'Client', 'Model', 'Amount'. This tests whether the agent ignores irrelevant columns. I avoided renaming any header whose name appears as a common English word in the instruction (e.g., 'Value' is used purely as a column reference, not in the instruction text as general English, so it's safe to rename).
